title: pastinaca brachytaenia
intro: Pastinaca brachytaenia is a plant species in the carrot family, commonly known as the short-fruited hog's fennel. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic and expectorant effects, which help alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The plant's health benefits also include its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and ease pain. Traditionally, Pastinaca brachytaenia has been prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, often used to treat respiratory conditions and gastrointestinal issues.
